context: ing water and they've been getting worse, not better. There's a {quest_item} that would help.
full_text: {settlement_npc} took a bad fall hauling water and they've been getting worse, not better. There's a {quest_item} that would help. I know it would, I've seen it work before, but I haven't been able to leave their side long enough to find one. Please.

context: ing this way again. I want this finished, not delayed.
full_text: Now we put it down. {kill_count} of those {enemy_type_plural} should be enough to break their numbers and make them think twice about coming this way again. I want this finished, not delayed.

match: , not when I'm old and telling the story.
context: something this big. I won't forget that, not when I'm old and telling the story.
full_text: We did it. We actually did it, and it's better than I let myself imagine. Take {reward_item}, and know that you helped me believe I could finish something this big. I won't forget that, not when I'm old and telling the story.

match: , not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.
context: at that level. Not in {settlement_name}, not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.
full_text: You've heard of {target_npc}'s reputation, then? Good. Then you understand why I'm willing to send a stranger across the region just for a conversation. There's no one else within reach who's at that level. Not in {settlement_name}, not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.

match: , not alone, but I started it, and that counts.
context: nd now and I see something I made happen, not alone, but I started it, and that counts. Take {reward_item}, with my real and la
full_text: I look around now and I see something I made happen, not alone, but I started it, and that counts. Take {reward_item}, with my real and lasting thanks. When people ask me how this came together, your name comes up first, and I mean to tell that story every chance I get.

match: , not a trap, and you watch.
context: u set out bait that looks like a mistake, not a trap, and you watch.
full_text: Stores going missing with nothing broken? I had the exact same problem two seasons ago. It drove me half mad until I figured out the trick. You set out bait that looks like a mistake, not a trap, and you watch.

context: ell them both. The truth should end this, not keep it going.
full_text: Tell them both. The truth should end this, not keep it going.

context: and I think the accusation was the lever, not the cause. I haven't said it out loud because I do
full_text: There's a piece I haven't told anyone yet. The person who first pointed at my friend had reasons of their own to want them gone, and I think the accusation was the lever, not the cause. I haven't said it out loud because I don't have proof, but I will by the time this is over.

context: I need to show people, not just tell them. Bring me a {quest_item}. It's the kind
full_text: I need to show people, not just tell them. Bring me a {quest_item}. It's the kind of thing that would have been left behind if my version of events is right. If we can put it in front of the accusers, the story falls apart on its own.

context: I can't bring this to her myself. Not yet. I need you to find {target_npc} in {tar
full_text: I can't bring this to her myself. Not yet. I need you to find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They used to know us both, before all this. If anyone can tell her I'm trying, it's them.

match: Not worth dragging through the mud again.
context: It's an old story. Not worth dragging through the mud again.
full_text: It's an old story. Not worth dragging through the mud again.

context: hat she heard. I never meant it that way, not even close. But I see now how it sounded, and I can
full_text: {target_npc} actually told you? Oh. Oh, that's what she heard. I never meant it that way, not even close. But I see now how it sounded, and I can't unsay it.

match: Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks.
context: He slept last night. Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks. He's not back yet, and I don't know if
full_text: He slept last night. Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks. He's not back yet, and I don't know if he's going to be the same when he is, but he's reachable now, and that's everything. Please take {reward_item}, offered by a wife who had run out of hope and has some back, because you brought my husband one step closer to home.

context: grieving, then she needs something back, not more emptiness. Find me a {quest_item} that feels like
full_text: If she's grieving, then she needs something back, not more emptiness. Find me a {quest_item} that feels like a beginning, something a person uses when they're starting again, not finishing. I'll bring it to her and tell her it's from someone who cares about her.

context: person uses when they're starting again, not finishing. I'll bring it to her and tell her it's
full_text: If she's grieving, then she needs something back, not more emptiness. Find me a {quest_item} that feels like a beginning, something a person uses when they're starting again, not finishing. I'll bring it to her and tell her it's from someone who cares about her.

match: , not all the way, but it's not worse anymore.
context: t with me for an hour. That's not better, not all the way, but it's not worse anymore. {reward_item} is yours, with the kind o
full_text: She came over for tea this morning. She didn't talk about her partner, but she didn't talk about giving things away either, and she sat with me for an hour. That's not better, not all the way, but it's not worse anymore. {reward_item} is yours, with the kind of thanks I'll still mean a year from now when I'm remembering this week. You helped me reach her when I couldn't reach her on my own.

context: She needs something given back, not more taken away. Something small that says the world isn
full_text: She needs something given back, not more taken away. Something small that says the world isn't done with her yet.

match: , not stuck in {settlement_name} listening to the wind.
context: want to spend his last days at his side, not stuck in {settlement_name} listening to the wind.
full_text: My brother is dying. I haven't been able to get to him because the {enemy_type_plural} have been thick around here and I'm not the fighter I used to be. I need {kill_count} of them dealt with. I want to spend his last days at his side, not stuck in {settlement_name} listening to the wind.

match: , not a letter that arrives too late.
context: nyone's. He should hear it from a person, not a letter that arrives too late. Find {target_npc_role} and tell them pl
full_text: Her brother {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. They haven't spoken in years, my fault as much as anyone's. He should hear it from a person, not a letter that arrives too late. Find {target_npc_role} and tell them plainly.

match: Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief.
context: Of course. Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief. I'll be all right either way.
full_text: Of course. Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief. I'll be all right either way.

context: t to. Take {kill_count} of them down so I can breathe again.
full_text: I lost my son to {enemy_type_plural} a season ago. I'm not asking for revenge. I'm past that. But every time I see another one of them slinking around {settlement_name}, I feel like the world hasn't moved on, and I need it to. Take {kill_count} of them down so I can breathe again.

context: It's a different quiet now. Not better, exactly, but different. I caught myself thinking about somethin
full_text: It's a different quiet now. Not better, exactly, but different. I caught myself thinking about something other than him for a whole afternoon, and that felt like a small mercy. There's something else I'd like to ask, while I still have the courage.

match: I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now
context: s were old enough to remember properly. I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now I wonder if I made him afraid of the wro
full_text: His grandfather was killed by {enemy_type_plural} too, before any of us were old enough to remember properly. I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now I wonder if I made him afraid of the wrong things.

context: . It's the kind of thing you plan around, not improvise.
full_text: I've got a project in mind that could benefit the whole settlement, and I'm ready to start. I need a {quest_item} first. It's the kind of thing you plan around, not improvise.

context: ed out would open things up for everyone, not just me.
full_text: {enemy_type_plural} have been settled in along the route I'd normally take. {kill_count} of them cleared out would open things up for everyone, not just me.

match: , not my failing mind, just a creature with poor manners.
context: ms, droppings in the corner. Not a ghost, not my failing mind, just a creature with poor manners. Now I need to seal the gap.
full_text: There it is. Scratches on the beams, droppings in the corner. Not a ghost, not my failing mind, just a creature with poor manners. Now I need to seal the gap.

context: est_item} as a gesture. Something useful, not sentimental. The kind of thing that says I'm done ar
full_text: I'd like to bring a {quest_item} as a gesture. Something useful, not sentimental. The kind of thing that says I'm done arguing and I'd rather be neighbors again. Find me one, would you?

match: Not very interesting from the outside.
context: It's a family thing. Not very interesting from the outside.
full_text: It's a family thing. Not very interesting from the outside.

context: y will be looked after. That's a promise, not a courtesy.
full_text: Tell them the boy will be looked after. That's a promise, not a courtesy.

context: I'm fine. It's them I'm worried about, not me.
full_text: I'm fine. It's them I'm worried about, not me.

context: done the first time. Support from behind, not from in front.
full_text: Perfect. I'll hand it to my cousin and step back. That's what I should have done the first time. Support from behind, not from in front.

context: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. They'll understand what it means, and i
full_text: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. They'll understand what it means, and if they don't, it's still a nice thing to receive. Either way, I've said what I needed to say.

match: Not too much, not too little.
context: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. They'll understand what it means, and i
full_text: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. They'll understand what it means, and if they don't, it's still a nice thing to receive. Either way, I've said what I needed to say.

context: work rotation, and I can't afford that. Not with what I owe.
full_text: I need a {quest_item} and I need it soon. I hurt my back three days ago and I've been hiding it because if {settlement_npc} finds out they'll pull me from the work rotation, and I can't afford that. Not with what I owe.

match: , not the twine and prayer I've been using.
context: _item} for the repairs. The real repairs, not the twine and prayer I've been using. If I can get the perimeter solid again
full_text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} for the repairs. The real repairs, not the twine and prayer I've been using. If I can get the perimeter solid again I might actually sleep tonight.

context: e else walked that path. There was blood, not much, but enough. I've been telling everyone my cousin is
full_text: You saw the scrub marks on the trail stones, didn't you. I cleaned up what I found this morning before anyone else walked that path. There was blood, not much, but enough. I've been telling everyone my cousin is just delayed, and I need that to stay true for a little longer.

context: nly, without hedging, and I trust them. I can breathe again.
full_text: {target_npc} says the worst has passed. They said it plainly, without hedging, and I trust them. I can breathe again.

match: , not the kind I've been doing with rope and optimism.
context: e repairs. The kind of repair that lasts, not the kind I've been doing with rope and optimism. Bring me that and the road opens up aga
full_text: I need a {quest_item} to make the repairs. The kind of repair that lasts, not the kind I've been doing with rope and optimism. Bring me that and the road opens up again.

context: p them from coming back. A permanent fix, not another temporary reprieve.
full_text: Go see {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role} who dealt with this exact problem in their own settlement last year. I want to know what they did to keep them from coming back. A permanent fix, not another temporary reprieve.

context: s rebuilt, the formulas are tested, and I can work again without second-guessing every measureme
full_text: The archive is rebuilt, the formulas are tested, and I can work again without second-guessing every measurement. Take {reward_item}, the first thing I made with my hand steady and my formulas back where they belong. You helped me recover something that fire couldn't kill, only scatter.

match: , not the pit fire I've been using.
context: I want to build a kiln. A proper one, not the pit fire I've been using. Every craftworker in {settlement_name}
full_text: I want to build a kiln. A proper one, not the pit fire I've been using. Every craftworker in {settlement_name} has to send their glazework to {other_settlement} because we can't fire it here, and I'm tired of watching our coin leave with every shipment. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} for the foundation.

context: l would say no. Too expensive, too risky, not enough demand. So I'm building it on my own plot with
full_text: You're wondering why I didn't mention this to the council first. Because {settlement_npc} told me the council would say no. Too expensive, too risky, not enough demand. So I'm building it on my own plot with my own materials, and I'll present them with a working kiln instead of a proposal they can reject.

context: the survey work itself. Proper equipment, not guesswork. If I'm going to claim I found something
full_text: One last thing. I need a {quest_item} for the survey work itself. Proper equipment, not guesswork. If I'm going to claim I found something, I want the measurements to back it up.

context: because I wanted you to see the ambition, not the longing.
full_text: You saw the drawings on the wall, didn't you. Those aren't just plans. They're dreams I've been sketching since I was twelve years old, sitting in the back of someone else's forge and wondering if I'd ever have my own. I didn't tell you that because I wanted you to see the ambition, not the longing.

match: , not chased off to come back tomorrow.
context: ll_count} of them put down at the source, not chased off to come back tomorrow.
full_text: I know where they den. I've known for weeks. The {enemy_type_plural} that have been raiding us aren't scattered, they're organized, and they come from the same hollow every time. I want {kill_count} of them put down at the source, not chased off to come back tomorrow.

context: ference in the air. But I want certainty, not hope.
full_text: The den is broken. I can feel the difference in the air. But I want certainty, not hope.

context: rms? That's coordinated hunting behavior, not scavenging. From the bite patterns you're describin
full_text: Livestock kills across multiple farms? That's coordinated hunting behavior, not scavenging. From the bite patterns you're describing, I'd say you have a pack that's shifted its territory. Hit them where they rest during the day and hit them together.

match: , not pretty, not finished, but standing.
context: The workshop is standing, not pretty, not finished, but standing. Take {reward_item}. I found one jar in
full_text: The workshop is standing, not pretty, not finished, but standing. Take {reward_item}. I found one jar in the rubble that came through without a crack, and I've been keeping it on the new shelf because it reminds me what you did. You showed up when the ground was still shaking, and that's the kind of thing a person remembers for a long time.

context: at's gone makes you feel it more clearly, not less. Now comes the part I've been holding in
full_text: I set it at the edge of where the oldest tree used to stand. Funny how marking what's gone makes you feel it more clearly, not less. Now comes the part I've been holding in my teeth for months.

context: ld. I am not watching that happen twice. Not with her garden.
full_text: I want {kill_count} of those {enemy_type_plural} gone. They'll come back otherwise, and they'll do to the new growth what they did to the old. I am not watching that happen twice. Not with her garden.

match: Not rationally, but in that part of your mind that keeps count.
context: s exactly how it feels, like I cause it. Not rationally, but in that part of your mind that keeps count. Everything I touch seems to sour eventu
full_text: It's funny you put it that way, because that's exactly how it feels, like I cause it. Not rationally, but in that part of your mind that keeps count. Everything I touch seems to sour eventually and I've stopped starting new things because I'm afraid I'll ruin them the way I've ruined everything else. Saying it out loud sounds foolish, but there it is.

context: ars and nothing came up. Not thin growth, not poor yield. Nothing. I need a {quest_item} to test
full_text: I planted in the same field I've used for twelve years and nothing came up. Not thin growth, not poor yield. Nothing. I need a {quest_item} to test the soil and find out whether the ground has given up on me or whether something else is at work.

match: Not thin growth, not poor yield.
context: ed for twelve years and nothing came up. Not thin growth, not poor yield. Nothing. I need a {quest_item} to test
full_text: I planted in the same field I've used for twelve years and nothing came up. Not thin growth, not poor yield. Nothing. I need a {quest_item} to test the soil and find out whether the ground has given up on me or whether something else is at work.

context: t me, and you showed me it was just sick, not hostile. That difference matters more than you k
full_text: Twelve years of good harvests, one bad one, and now the start of recovery. Take {reward_item}, and come back when the field bears again so I can show you what it was you saved. I was ready to believe the land had turned against me, and you showed me it was just sick, not hostile. That difference matters more than you know.

context: he pile is getting impressive, honestly. Not in a good way.
full_text: Then I add it to the pile. The pile is getting impressive, honestly. Not in a good way.

context: Solved. Actually solved, not patched, not postponed. The channel holds, the shelves are full
full_text: Solved. Actually solved, not patched, not postponed. The channel holds, the shelves are full, and the road is safe. I keep waiting for the next thing to fail and it hasn't.

context: t's broken. The whole operation needs it, not only the fence. He stripped the place on his way out, a
full_text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to patch what's broken. The whole operation needs it, not only the fence. He stripped the place on his way out, and I've been pretending it doesn't matter.

context: hey looked at me like I was the stranger, not them. Like I was the one who didn't belong in
full_text: All right, since you're not going to let me dodge this. I tried to stop them last week by grabbing their hands. They looked at me like I was the stranger, not them. Like I was the one who didn't belong in this house.

context: ost beautiful thing I've heard in weeks. Not cured, I know. But managed means she stays.
full_text: {target_npc} said it can be managed? That word, managed, is the most beautiful thing I've heard in weeks. Not cured, I know. But managed means she stays.

context: eone to talk to. That confirms it's real, not something his mind invented. Knowing that changes everything.
full_text: So {target_npc} saw something out there too, and they're managing it better because they had someone to talk to. That confirms it's real, not something his mind invented. Knowing that changes everything.

context: nnels? I designed them to last ten years, not twenty. Whoever's been adapting them has a good
full_text: Someone is still maintaining those channels? I designed them to last ten years, not twenty. Whoever's been adapting them has a good sense of how water moves. The adjustments are better than what I'd have suggested myself.

match: Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking.
context: He's at the workbench this morning. Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking. {reward_item} is yours, handed over by
full_text: He's at the workbench this morning. Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking. {reward_item} is yours, handed over by a brother who had nothing to offer his own brother for weeks and is finally able to offer something to someone. You helped me reach a person I couldn't reach alone, and that's a debt I'll carry gladly.

context: n able to tell anyone is that I'm angry. Not at her.
full_text: You have a warmth about you that makes difficult things feel possible. I need that right now. The thing I haven't been able to tell anyone is that I'm angry. Not at her.

match: Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture.
context: That's close. Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture. I put it where hers used to sit, and th
full_text: That's close. Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture. I put it where hers used to sit, and the room feels less empty now.

match: , not one of the best, the best.
context: st {self_role} this region has ever seen, not one of the best, the best. That starts with materials, and I need
full_text: I've decided I'm going to be the best {self_role} this region has ever seen, not one of the best, the best. That starts with materials, and I need {gather_count} {quest_item} of the finest quality I can get. No excuses, no substitutions.

match: Not everyone can see what I'm building.
context: I expected more, honestly. Not everyone can see what I'm building. That's fine. I'll remember who showed u
full_text: I expected more, honestly. Not everyone can see what I'm building. That's fine. I'll remember who showed up and who didn't.

context: them to see someone who solves problems, not someone who asks permission.
full_text: Perfect. When I walk into that room, I want them to see someone who solves problems, not someone who asks permission.

context: item} to fund the proposal I'm bringing. Not gifts, investments. I want the council to see that I've alr
full_text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to fund the proposal I'm bringing. Not gifts, investments. I want the council to see that I've already started the work, that I'm showing them I've been sitting in that seat already, whether they gave it to me or not.

context: it matched the clay near Margel's house, not the road to {other_settlement}. The excuses don't hold up and I can see
full_text: You noticed the hole in what I'm asking you to do. I already have the answer. I checked the mud on their boots three days ago and it matched the clay near Margel's house, not the road to {other_settlement}. The excuses don't hold up and I can see that plainly. But knowing wasn't enough to stop the feeling, and the feeling has its own logic that ignores evidence entirely.

context: he council made its decision on my anger, not on facts.
full_text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} handled the original inspection when the complaint went through. They'll remember what they saw. Ask them to look at the evidence again, because I think the council made its decision on my anger, not on facts.

context: That trader was the one moving goods out, not some child.
full_text: I'd swear to it in front of anyone. That trader was the one moving goods out, not some child.

context: ks that the contamination was structural, not deliberate. But I still need to find the traveler,
full_text: Good. With this I can show anyone who asks that the contamination was structural, not deliberate. But I still need to find the traveler, or at least send word that they were wrongly accused.

match: Not all of them, but enough.
context: finally willing to admit we were wrong. Not all of them, but enough. Take {reward_item}, and I hope it count
full_text: The well is repaired, the word is out, and a few people in {settlement_name} are finally willing to admit we were wrong. Not all of them, but enough. Take {reward_item}, and I hope it counts for something on a day when nothing I give quite balances what we did. You helped me make right what I could, even if I can't undo all of it.

context: when I said it and the words were chosen, not thrown. That's the part that doesn't fit the ve
full_text: You picked up on something I left out, didn't you. I called it an argument, like we were both fighting, but I wasn't angry. I was calm when I said it and the words were chosen, not thrown. That's the part that doesn't fit the version I've been telling, because there was no heat and I just wanted to win.

context: That's good. Something useful, not sentimental. My friend doesn't need more feelings ri
full_text: That's good. Something useful, not sentimental. My friend doesn't need more feelings right now, they need a reason to stop hiding. This gives them one.

context: ting my own picture on it. That's vanity, not encouragement.
full_text: You have an ease about you that makes me want to drop the pretense, so I will. I misread the situation on purpose, because I wanted to be the person who brought two people together and liked the idea of seeing what nobody else could see. My friend's heart was the canvas and I was painting my own picture on it. That's vanity, not encouragement.

context: kept alive by stubbornness at this point, not by any real grievance. The older generation holds the line out
full_text: {target_npc} says the feud is mostly kept alive by stubbornness at this point, not by any real grievance. The older generation holds the line out of habit, and the younger ones follow because nobody's given them permission to stop. That gives me hope, thin hope, but real.

match: Not a formal endorsement, just an honest word.
context: st speak on behalf of the one proposing. Not a formal endorsement, just an honest word. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}
full_text: The family has a tradition that someone outside the courtship must speak on behalf of the one proposing. Not a formal endorsement, just an honest word. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} has known me since I was young. Ask them if they'd be willing to say a few words about my character when the time comes.

context: t preparing. The hard part is the asking, not the answer.
full_text: Tell them to stop worrying and start preparing. The hard part is the asking, not the answer.

context: an frame it as an addition to the circle, not a break from tradition. Bring me a {quest_item} they've made so
full_text: The person I love makes a particular kind of {quest_item} that nobody in the trade circle produces. If I can show my family that this person brings a skill we're missing, I can frame it as an addition to the circle, not a break from tradition. Bring me a {quest_item} they've made so I can present it.

match: , not to keep good people out.
context: dition exists to keep our standards high, not to keep good people out.
full_text: I've led this trade circle long enough to know when the rules serve us and when we serve the rules. There have been outsiders welcomed before, three times in my memory, and each time the circle was stronger for it. The tradition exists to keep our standards high, not to keep good people out.

context: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. But I realize now I can't just send it
full_text: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. But I realize now I can't just send it without any context. I need someone to prepare the ground.

match: Not too much, not too little.
context: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. But I realize now I can't just send it
full_text: That's exactly right. Not too much, not too little. But I realize now I can't just send it without any context. I need someone to prepare the ground.

context: en through {other_settlement} recently. That's not nothing. That's an opening, and I've been too s
full_text: So {target_npc} says this person has mentioned me more than once, and always warmly. They asked about my work, my health, whether I'd been through {other_settlement} recently. That's not nothing. That's an opening, and I've been too scared to step through it.

context: t the traveling itself could be the life, not the obstacle to one. We built a routine around the road, and
full_text: I know that problem well. I spent five years going back and forth between two places, and for the first two I was miserable because I kept thinking I had to pick one. What changed was realizing that the traveling itself could be the life, not the obstacle to one. We built a routine around the road, and the road became home in its own way.

context: Aye, that's fair. It's my ghost, not yours. I'll carry it a while longer.
full_text: Aye, that's fair. It's my ghost, not yours. I'll carry it a while longer.

context: day he said I was ready, and I never was, not really. But I think he'd want it with someone w
full_text: Here, take {reward_item}. He gave it to me the day he said I was ready, and I never was, not really. But I think he'd want it with someone who finished the thing he couldn't. I'll visit his marker this week and tell him about you.

match: , not with old widows who stare at them.
context: d things belong with people who use them, not with old widows who stare at them. The season is turning, and for the firs
full_text: Take {reward_item}, please. It was his, and he always said good things belong with people who use them, not with old widows who stare at them. The season is turning, and for the first time in a year I'm not dreading it.

context: ares. She doesn't need to know different, not yet. Just help me finish what I started, fri
full_text: I've been fortifying the house for months now, and I'm close to having it done. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to finish the last of it, and my hands are shaking too much lately to go gather it myself. My wife thinks I'm out checking snares. She doesn't need to know different, not yet. Just help me finish what I started, friend, and I'll tell you the rest after.

context: can't show them a father who's breaking. Not yet.
